claims.claim_text: Wastewater treatment plants (WWTPs) represent a distinctive class of behind-the-meter industrial loads where electrical demand is dominated by aeration blowers that maintain dissolved oxygen levels for biological nutrient removal.

Enrollment in an ISO demand response (DR) program creates a revenue opportunity through capacity payments tied to guaranteed load-shed commitments, yet curtailing aeration power introduces a process-level risk: if ammonia or total nitrogen concentrations exceed state-formula effluent limits, the facility incurs regulatory fines whose magnitude scales with the duration and severity of the exceedance.
source_chunk_ids: Inputs · REopt.jl Documentation_Used with Health Options 1 or 2: Annual percent decrease in health emission_0_17

claims.claim_text: The engineering-economic optimization task addressed in this work is to size a behind-the-meter microgrid—comprising battery energy storage (BESS), solar PV, and a backup diesel generator—such that the facility maximizes net present value (NPV) across the full set of additive cash-flow components that arise from this dual participation.

The REopt optimization engine minimizes life cycle cost (equivalently maximizing NPV) by selecting optimal DER sizes and hourly dispatch subject to load-balance, technology, and financial constraints.
The Financial case reported by REopt 'minimizes the present value of all future energy costs over the analysis period' and 'may include a combination of utility, PV, wind, CHP, GHP… and/or battery'.

NPV is computed as the difference between the Business-as-Usual life cycle cost and the optimized-case life cycle cost, where the life cycle cost encompasses 'utility demand and energy costs as well as the capital expenditure, tax benefits and incentives, and O&M costs associated with the project'.
REopt's hourly dispatch co-optimizes PV production, battery charge/discharge, and generator operation against the site's tariff structure, including both volumetric energy charges and demand charges.
Battery sizing is driven in part by demand-charge reduction: 'for batteries, high demand charges are important for economic viability'.

The BESS is parameterized with independent power (kW) and energy (kWh) capacities, installed cost decomposed into $/kW, $/kWh, and a constant term, round-trip AC-AC efficiency defaulting to 89.9%, and a minimum state-of-charge floor of 20% during grid-connected operation.
source_chunk_ids: Inputs · REopt.jl Documentation_Used with Health Options 1 or 2: Annual percent decrease in health emission_0_17

claims.claim_text: PV is modeled via PVWatts production factors with default system losses of 14% and a DC/AC ratio of 1.2.
The backup generator defaults to an installed cost of $500/kW for grid-connected resilience applications with a full-load electric efficiency of 32.2% and diesel fuel cost of $2.25/gal.
source_chunk_ids: Inputs · REopt.jl Documentation_The replacement strategy cost is:_0_20

claims.claim_text: DR payments are not natively optimized within the REopt.jl v0.57 objective function. Instead, they are incorporated through post-processing.

REopt natively supports a financial input `value_of_lost_load_per_kwh` that, when used with multiple-outage modeling, causes 'the expected outage cost [to] be included in the net present value and lifecycle cost calculations'.
source_chunk_ids: Inputs · REopt.jl Documentation_Used with Health Options 1 or 2: Annual percent decrease in health emission_0_17

claims.claim_text: VoLL is supplied as an 8760-element hourly array produced by the external MATLAB routine `compute_voll_for_reopt.m`, which couples a biokinetic process simulator (ASM-family) with the state penalty formula to yield a time-varying $/kWh series that reflects seasonal influent loading, temperature-dependent nitrification kinetics, and the nonlinear relationship between aeration curtailment duration and effluent NH4/TN breakthrough.
source_chunk_ids: Inputs · REopt.jl Documentation_Used with Health Options 1 or 2: Annual percent decrease in health emission_0_17

claims.claim_text: The REopt documentation confirms that the model 'minimize[s] the maximum expected outage cost' when outage start time steps and durations are provided, and that setting `value_of_lost_load_per_kwh` to zero removes the incentive for the model to meet critical loads during outages.
source_chunk_ids: Inputs · REopt.jl Documentation_Used with Health Options 1 or 2: Annual percent decrease in health emission_0_17
Inputs · REopt.jl Documentation_minimax the expected outage cost, with max taken over outage start time, ex_0_9

claims.claim_text: A non-trivial VoLL array directly steers DER sizing toward configurations that hedge against aeration-curtailment-induced fines.
source_chunk_ids: Inputs · REopt.jl Documentation_Used with Health Options 1 or 2: Annual percent decrease in health emission_0_17

claims.claim_text: All technology capital expenditures enter the life cycle cost as present-value sums inclusive of federal Investment Tax Credit (default 30%), MACRS accelerated depreciation (5-year schedule with 100% bonus), and state/utility rebates where applicable.
source_chunk_ids: Inputs · REopt.jl Documentation_Used with Health Options 1 or 2: Annual percent decrease in health emission_0_17

claims.claim_text: Annual O&M costs escalate at the inflation rate (default 2.5%) and are discounted over the analysis period (default 25 years) at the nominal discount rate (default 6.24%).
Battery replacement cost is modeled at a user-specified replacement year (default year 10), with the replacement cost present-valued and added to the objective.
source_chunk_ids: Inputs · REopt.jl Documentation_Used with Health Options 1 or 2: Annual percent decrease in health emission_0_17
Inputs · REopt.jl Documentation_The replacement strategy cost is:_0_20

claims.claim_text: The replacement strategy cost follows C_repl = B_kWh · N_repl · f(d_80) · C_install, where f(d_80) is the present worth factor at the month the state-of-health falls below 80%.
source_chunk_ids: Inputs · REopt.jl Documentation_The replacement strategy cost is:_0_20

claims.claim_text: Islanding capability requires additional switchgear, controls, and protection equipment. REopt parameterizes this through `microgrid_upgrade_cost_fraction`, a fractional adder applied to recommended DER capital costs.
source_chunk_ids: Inputs · REopt.jl Documentation_Used with Health Options 1 or 2: Annual percent decrease in health emission_0_17

claims.claim_text: PV array capacity (kW_DC) is bounded by available roof and ground area converted via default power densities of 0.01 kW/ft² (rooftop) and 6 × 10⁻³ acres/kW (ground mount).
source_chunk_ids: Inputs · REopt.jl Documentation_Used with Health Options 1 or 2: Annual percent decrease in health emission_0_17

claims.claim_text: BESS power capacity (kW_AC) and energy capacity (kWh) are independently optimized without a predefined power-to-energy ratio.
Backup generator capacity (kW) has constraints on minimum turndown fraction (default 0% for grid-connected resilience, 15% for off-grid) and finite fuel availability.
source_chunk_ids: Inputs · REopt.jl Documentation_The replacement strategy cost is:_0_20

claims.claim_text: The model treats technology sizes as continuous variables; commercially available discrete sizes are addressed in post-processing.
Energy arbitrage optimization beyond what emerges endogenously from time-of-use rate structures in the tariff is explicitly excluded from the formulation.
Voltage support and power quality services are explicitly excluded, as they would require distribution-network power-flow models outside REopt's load-balance formulation.
Ancillary service co-optimization (e.g., frequency regulation, spinning reserve markets) is excluded, as it would demand sub-hourly dispatch resolution and market-clearing co-simulation.

Pareto-style multi-objective formulations that would trade off NPV against resilience hours, emissions, or process reliability as independent objectives are excluded; instead the current approach monetizes process risk through the VoLL array and collapses all objectives into a single NPV scalar.

The resilience modeling capability of REopt—where the system is 'optimized to sustain a critical load in the event of a grid outage while minimizing the present value of all future energy costs'—provides the mathematical infrastructure for enforcing minimum load-service constraints during DR commitment windows.

The Energy Resilience Performance (ERP) post-processing tool uses a Markovian reliability model incorporating Mean Time to Failure, operational availability, and failure-to-start probabilities for each DER type (Marqusee et al., 2021) and can further quantify the probability that the sized microgrid sustains aeration loads across all possible DR event start times.
source_chunk_ids: Methods · REopt.jl Documentation_Return dic!onary of backup reliability results._0_3

claims.claim_text: The NPV formulation aggregates five principal terms: PV of avoided energy, PV of DR capacity, PV of fine penalty, PV of DER CapEx+O&M, and PV of microgrid upgrade.
The microgrid's ability to shed grid load in any hour t equals the instantaneous surplus of on-site generation plus available battery discharge over the critical process load floor.
When the microgrid cannot fully displace grid-drawn aeration power during a DR event, biological treatment performance degrades and the probability of an effluent exceedance rises.
source_chunk_ids: Inputs · REopt.jl Documentation_Used with Health Options 1 or 2: Annual percent decrease in health emission_0_17

claims.claim_text: The microgrid upgrade cost is incurred only when the system must operate islanded during DR events or grid outages, and it enters the life cycle cost alongside technology-specific CapEx.
source_chunk_ids: Inputs · REopt.jl Documentation_Used with Health Options 1 or 2: Annual percent decrease in health emission_0_17

claims.claim_text: The optimization simultaneously determines PV array capacity, BESS power and energy capacity, backup generator capacity, and hourly dispatch of all assets across 8760 time steps.

introduction.summary_abstract: This study formulates the optimal sizing of a behind-the-meter microgrid (battery storage, solar PV, and backup diesel generation) for wastewater treatment plants participating in demand response programs while facing regulatory fine exposure from aeration curtailment. The net present value objective function integrates avoided energy costs, DR capacity revenue, expected effluent-violation penalties mediated by a time-varying Value of Lost Load derived from biokinetic process simulation, and DER capital and O&M costs within the REopt optimization framework.

violations.description: Stated word_count (1520) does not match actual word count (1317) of content_markdown.

claims.claim_text: The REopt.jl optimization engine consumes a structured JSON scenario file comprising distinct input blocks, each mapping to a set of parameters that populate the underlying mixed-integer linear program (MILP).
source_chunk_ids: 1. Structure of the REopt API · NatLabRockies:REopt_API Wiki_Django Models and Input Validation_0_57

claims.claim_text: The Site block's latitude and longitude fields drive downstream resource lookups including solar irradiance via PVWatts, wind via the Wind Toolkit, and emissions factors from Cambium/AVERT.
source_chunk_ids: Inputs · REopt.jl Documentation_Used with Health Options 1 or 2: Annual percent decrease in health emission_0_17

claims.claim_text: The field min_resil_time_steps is the hard resilience constraint: it specifies the minimum number of contiguous time steps for which the optimized DER portfolio must sustain the critical load without grid supply.
source_chunk_ids: Inputs · REopt.jl Documentation_Used with Health Options 1 or 2: Annual percent decrease in health emission_0_17

claims.claim_text: To ensure that REopt recommends a system that can meet critical loads during a defined outage period, specify this duration using Site | min_resil_time_steps.
source_chunk_ids: Inputs · REopt.jl Documentation_Used with Health Options 1 or 2: Annual percent decrease in health emission_0_17

claims.claim_text: When the stochastic multi-outage model is active, the optimizer may otherwise choose to shed load if doing so is cost-optimal; min_resil_time_steps overrides this trade-off by imposing an infeasibility on any solution that cannot island for the specified duration.
source_chunk_ids: Inputs · REopt.jl Documentation_Used with Health Options 1 or 2: Annual percent decrease in health emission_0_17

claims.claim_text: The Financial block's key fields include analysis_years (default 25), offtaker_discount_rate_fraction, and microgrid_upgrade_cost_fraction, which scales the capital cost of switchgear and controls required to island DER assets.
The field value_of_lost_load_per_kwh (VoLL) is the primary channel through which the hourly willingness-to-pay for reliability enters the MILP.
source_chunk_ids: Inputs · REopt.jl Documentation_Used with Health Options 1 or 2: Annual percent decrease in health emission_0_17

claims.claim_text: In the stochastic outage formulation, expected outage cost is computed as the product of unserved energy, the per-kWh VoLL, and the probability weight of each outage scenario; this term appears directly in the objective function alongside lifecycle energy costs.
source_chunk_ids: Inputs · REopt.jl Documentation_Used with Health Options 1 or 2: Annual percent decrease in health emission_0_17

claims.claim_text: The documentation confirms that expected outage cost will be included in the net present value and lifecycle cost calculations (for both the BAU and optimized case) and that setting VoLL to zero will remove incentive for the model to meet critical loads during outages.
source_chunk_ids: Inputs · REopt.jl Documentation_Used with Health Options 1 or 2: Annual percent decrease in health emission_0_17

claims.claim_text: The ElectricLoad block accepts either an annual_kwh scalar paired with a doe_reference_name (selecting from DOE Commercial Reference Building profiles) or a custom 8760 (or sub-hourly) loads_kw time series.
The critical_load_fraction parameter sets the fraction of the total electric load that constitutes the non-negotiable demand floor during grid outages.

During islanded operation, the optimizer must dispatch DER to meet at least critical_load_fraction × loads_kw[t] in every outage time step t; any deficit up to this floor is penalized at the VoLL rate (or rendered infeasible if min_resil_time_steps binds).
source_chunk_ids: Inputs · REopt.jl Documentation_Used with Health Options 1 or 2: Annual percent decrease in health emission_0_17

claims.claim_text: The Resilience case is optimized to sustain a critical load in the event of a grid outage while minimizing the present value of all future energy costs over the analysis period.

The ElectricTariff block has two primary specification modes: a simplified representation via blended_annual_energy_rate ($/kWh) and blended_annual_demand_rate ($/kW/month), or a detailed tariff referenced by urdb_label from the Utility Rate Database.
source_chunk_ids: 1. Structure of the REopt API · NatLabRockies:REopt_API Wiki_Django Models and Input Validation_0_57

claims.claim_text: For deterministic single-outage analyses, outage_start_time_step and outage_end_time_step define a fixed islanding window.
source_chunk_ids: Inputs · REopt.jl Documentation_Single Outage Modeling Inputs (Outage Modeling Option 1):_0_7

claims.claim_text: For the stochastic multi-outage formulation, three parallel arrays drive the ExpectedOutageCost term in the objective: outage_start_time_steps, outage_durations, and outage_probabilities.
source_chunk_ids: Inputs · REopt.jl Documentation_Used with Health Options 1 or 2: Annual percent decrease in health emission_0_17

claims.claim_text: Cannot supply singular outage_start(or end)_time_step and multiple outage_start_time_steps. Must use one or the other.
source_chunk_ids: Inputs · REopt.jl Documentation_Used with Health Options 1 or 2: Annual percent decrease in health emission_0_17

claims.claim_text: When stochastic outages are modeled, emissions and renewable energy percentage constraints do not factor in outage periods.
source_chunk_ids: Inputs · REopt.jl Documentation_Used with Health Options 1 or 2: Annual percent decrease in health emission_0_17

claims.claim_text: The ElectricStorage block optimizer selects both power capacity (kW) and energy capacity (kWh) within user-specified bounds: min_kw, max_kw (default 1 × 10^4), min_kwh, and max_kwh (default 1 × 10^6).
source_chunk_ids: Inputs · REopt.jl Documentation_Used with Health Options 1 or 2: Annual percent decrease in health emission_0_17

claims.claim_text: ElectricStorage additional parameters govern round-trip efficiency (inverter_efficiency_fraction, rectifier_efficiency_fraction, internal_efficiency_fraction), minimum state-of-charge (soc_min_fraction, default 0.2), and whether grid charging is permitted (can_grid_charge).
source_chunk_ids: Inputs · REopt.jl Documentation_Used with Health Options 1 or 2: Annual percent decrease in health emission_0_17

claims.claim_text: ElectricStorage installed costs follow a two-part structure: installed_cost_per_kw ($968 default for power electronics) and installed_cost_per_kwh ($253 default for cell modules), plus a fixed constant.
source_chunk_ids: Inputs · REopt.jl Documentation_Used with Health Options 1 or 2: Annual percent decrease in health emission_0_17

claims.claim_text: The soc_min_applies_during_outages boolean controls whether the SOC floor relaxes during islanded operation; the Energy Resilience Performance tool post-processor assumes full depth-of-discharge during outages regardless of this setting.
If no production_factor_series is provided for PV, REopt calls NREL's PVWatts via the site coordinates to generate hourly capacity factors.
source_chunk_ids: Inputs · REopt.jl Documentation_Used with Health Options 1 or 2: Annual percent decrease in health emission_0_17

claims.claim_text: PV cost defaults follow a size-class methodology tied to average load magnitude, with installed_cost_per_kw ranging from $1,239/kW (utility scale) to $2,783/kW (residential) based on 2024 ATB data.
The PV degradation_fraction (default 0.5%/year) is applied to produce a lifetime-averaged production profile.
source_chunk_ids: Inputs · REopt.jl Documentation_Used with Health Options 1 or 2: Annual percent decrease in health emission_0_17

claims.claim_text: During outages, PV output is available to serve critical loads only if the microgrid upgrade is purchased (controlled by the microgrid_upgrade_cost_fraction in the Financial block).
source_chunk_ids: Inputs · REopt.jl Documentation_Used with Health Options 1 or 2: Annual percent decrease in health emission_0_17

claims.claim_text: The Generator block's boolean only_runs_during_grid_outage (default true) restricts generator dispatch to islanded periods for emergency-only configurations.
source_chunk_ids: Inputs · REopt.jl Documentation_The replacement strategy cost is:_0_20

claims.claim_text: Generator fuel consumption follows a linear curve parameterized by the full-load and half-load efficiencies, consistent with the CHP fuel burn formulation described in the REopt documentation.
The Generator min_turn_down_fraction defaults to 0.0 for grid-connected resilience scenarios but can be adjusted to reflect operational constraints on partial loading.

Financial.value_of_lost_load_per_kwh consumes the hourly VoLL array and scales unserved energy into monetary cost within the objective function, serving as the mechanism through which heterogeneous, time-varying reliability valuations propagate into DER sizing decisions.
source_chunk_ids: Inputs · REopt.jl Documentation_Used with Health Options 1 or 2: Annual percent decrease in health emission_0_17

claims.claim_text: ElectricUtility.outage_start_time_steps / outage_durations / outage_probabilities together define the stochastic outage scenarios that generate the ExpectedOutageCost term, with the probability-weighted summation across scenarios ensuring the optimizer hedges against the full distribution of outage events rather than a single deterministic window.
source_chunk_ids: Inputs · REopt.jl Documentation_Used with Health Options 1 or 2: Annual percent decrease in health emission_0_17

claims.claim_text: ElectricLoad.critical_load_fraction establishes the hard floor for load that must be served during any outage; combined with Site.min_resil_time_steps, this parameter determines whether the resilience requirement manifests as a soft cost penalty (via VoLL) or a hard feasibility constraint.
source_chunk_ids: Inputs · REopt.jl Documentation_Used with Health Options 1 or 2: Annual percent decrease in health emission_0_17

claims.claim_text: The expected outage cost for the stochastic formulation is computed as ExpectedOutageCost = sum over scenarios s of p_s times the sum over time steps t of VoLL_t times UnservedLoad_{s,t}.
source_chunk_ids: Inputs · REopt.jl Documentation_Used with Health Options 1 or 2: Annual percent decrease in health emission_0_17

claims.claim_text: The default analysis period in the Financial block is 25 years.
For emergency-only configurations, the default assumption is that the generator only operates during grid outages.
VoLL sets the marginal price of reliability, the outage specification vectors define the threat landscape, and the critical load fraction determines the quantity of demand that must be defended.
source_chunk_ids: Inputs · REopt.jl Documentation_Used with Health Options 1 or 2: Annual percent decrease in health emission_0_17

scenario_components_overview.summary_abstract: REopt.jl consumes a structured JSON scenario comprising interdependent input blocks—Site, Financial, ElectricLoad, ElectricTariff, ElectricUtility, ElectricStorage, PV, and Generator—that parameterize a mixed-integer linear program for resilience-oriented DER sizing. Three parameters carry the core decision signals: `value_of_lost_load_per_kwh` introduces time-varying reliability valuations into the objective function, stochastic outage vectors (`outage_start_time_steps`, `outage_durations`, `outage_probabilities`) define the probability-weighted threat landscape, and `critical_load_fraction` establishes the non-negotiable demand floor during islanded operation.

Stated word_count (1420) does not match actual word count (1267) of content_markdown.

claims.claim_text: REopt.jl formulates distributed energy resource (DER) sizing and dispatch as a single-objective mixed-integer linear program (MILP) whose scalar objective minimizes the life-cycle cost (LCC) of energy over a user-specified analysis horizon.

The net present value (NPV) is defined as the difference between the business-as-usual (BAU) LCC and the optimized-case LCC, so minimizing LCC and maximizing NPV are algebraically equivalent; no Pareto multi-objective frontier is constructed.
The objective function assembles additive cost terms, each discounted to present value using the off-taker's nominal discount rate over `analysis_years` (default 25 years).
For each technology t in subdivision k, segment s, the capital cost is represented by a piecewise-linear function with slope c^{cm}_{ts} [$/kW] and intercept c^{cb}_{ts} [$].
Storage systems contribute two independent capital cost terms: c^{kW}_b [$/kW] for power capacity and c^{kWh}_b [$/kWh] for energy capacity.
Battery replacement or augmentation costs C_{repl} or C_{aug} are added to the objective when `model_degradation` is enabled.
source_chunk_ids: Inputs · REopt.jl Documentation_The replacement strategy cost is:_0_20

claims.claim_text: Both fixed O&M per unit of power rating (c^{omσ}_t [$/kW], which includes standby charges for CHP) and variable O&M per unit of production (c^{omp}_t [$/kWh]) are discounted over the analysis period using the present worth factor f_{om}.

Electricity purchases are valued at time-of-use energy prices c^{g}_{uh} [$/kWh] across tiered demand structures. Demand charges enter via monthly peak demand tiers (c^{rm}_{mn} [$/kW]) and time-of-use demand periods (c^{r}_{de} [$/kW]), with ratchet provisions over look-back months.
Fuel costs for on-site generation enter at c^{u}_f [$/MMBTU] discounted by technology-specific present worth factors f^{pf}_t.

When enabled, marginal costs of pollutant p from on-site fuel burn (c^{f}_p [$/ton]) and from grid-purchased electricity (c̄^{g}_p [$/ton]) are included, each with their own present worth factors f^{fc}_p and f^{gc}_p.
The expected outage cost term is weighted by `value_of_lost_load_per_kwh` (VoLL) and `outage_probabilities`, penalizing unserved energy during stochastic outage scenarios.
Energy export revenues at rates c^{e}_{uh} [$/kWh] across net-metering and wholesale tiers are subtracted from cost in the objective.
Production incentives X^{pi}_t [$] are bounded by technology-specific caps ī_t and power limits ī^{σ}_t.

The ITC reduces CAPEX directly (e.g., 30% for PV and storage meeting prevailing wage requirements), while MACRS bonus depreciation (up to 100% first-year under current law) reduces the depreciation basis by half the ITC value.
Battery residual value C_{residual}, computed as the present worth of remaining useful life at the end of the analysis period, is subtracted from total cost.
source_chunk_ids: Inputs · REopt.jl Documentation_The replacement strategy cost is:_0_20

claims.claim_text: ISO demand response (DR) capacity revenue is not native to the objective but is computed in post-processing, derived from the solved `ElectricStorage.size_kw` and `Generator.size_kw` capacities after the optimization terminates, and is reported as an ancillary economic metric rather than influencing DER sizing directly.
NPV is calculated as NPV = LCC_{BAU} - LCC_{Optimal}.
The BAU case includes only utility demand and energy costs, existing boiler fuel costs, and O&M for any existing PV or generator.
The optimized case adds CAPEX, tax benefits, incentives, and O&M for all recommended DER.
Fixed utility fees cancel in the NPV calculation because they cannot be offset by DER.

For financial analyses, NPV is guaranteed non-negative unless the user forces a minimum technology size; for resilience analyses, NPV may be negative, indicating that the resilience premium exceeds the economic benefit.
Avoided outage costs are not included in the NPV calculation by default, though adding them may increase NPV.
The stochastic outage formulation evaluates resilience across a combinatorial grid of `outage_start_time_steps` and `outage_durations`, each weighted by `outage_probabilities`.

The expected outage cost takes the form: ExpectedOutageCost = Σ_{s∈S} p_s Σ_{t∈T_s} VoLL_t · UnservedLoad_{s,t}, where p_s is the probability of outage scenario s and the inner summation runs over the time steps t within that scenario's duration.

The critical structural feature is a minimax arrangement: the maximum is taken over `outage_start_time_steps` (the optimizer must hedge against the worst-case start time), while the expectation is taken over `outage_durations` weighted by `outage_probabilities`.
Outage time-series results are stored as three-dimensional arrays indexed by [duration_scenario, start_time_step, timestep_within_outage].

The scenario JSON is loaded via `JSON.parsefile("path/to/scenario.json")`, which returns a nested `JSON.Object`. A documented bug requires recursive conversion of these objects to native Julia `Dict` types before passing them to REopt internals.
source_chunk_ids: 1. Structure of the REopt API · NatLabRockies:REopt_API Wiki_Django Models and Input Validation_0_57

claims.claim_text: The JuMP model is constructed with `m = Model(HiGHS.Optimizer)` and solver-level attributes are configured via `set_attribute(m, "time_limit", seconds)`.
source_chunk_ids: 1. Structure of the REopt API · NatLabRockies:REopt_API Wiki_Django Models and Input Validation_0_57

claims.claim_text: The REopt User Manual specifies default optimality tolerances of 0.1% for general cases, 1% for CHP scenarios, and 5% for off-grid analyses.

`run_reopt` supports two calling signatures: a single-model signature `results = run_reopt(m, scenario_dict)` that solves the optimized case only, and a two-model signature `results = run_reopt(m1, m2, scenario_dict)` that solves both the BAU and optimized cases in the same invocation, enabling automatic NPV calculation.
source_chunk_ids: 1. Structure of the REopt API · NatLabRockies:REopt_API Wiki_Django Models and Input Validation_0_57
1. Structure of the REopt API · NatLabRockies:REopt_API Wiki_The steps behind an optimization are:_0_56

claims.claim_text: The v3 architecture runs BAU and optimal scenarios in parallel within the Julia process, consolidating what were previously four separate Celery tasks into one.
source_chunk_ids: 1. Structure of the REopt API · NatLabRockies:REopt_API Wiki_Django Models and Input Validation_0_57

claims.claim_text: REopt returns results containing `JuMP.Containers.DenseAxisArray` objects, which are not directly JSON-serializable. A recursive `make_serializable` function must traverse the results dictionary, converting every `DenseAxisArray` to a standard Julia `Array` before calling `JSON.print(io, results)`.

Parametric studies such as sweeping `Financial.value_of_lost_load_per_kwh` follow a standardized pattern: deep copy the base scenario, override the target parameter, instantiate a fresh solver model per iteration, execute using the two-model signature, and collect and serialize results.
source_chunk_ids: 1. Structure of the REopt API · NatLabRockies:REopt_API Wiki_Django Models and Input Validation_0_57

claims.claim_text: The VoLL value may be a scalar or an 8,760-element (or sub-hourly) vector reflecting time-varying willingness-to-pay.

The `deepcopy`-override-solve-collect loop ensures each iteration operates on an independent scenario state, preventing cross-contamination of mutable dictionary references across parameter grid points.
source_chunk_ids: 1. Structure of the REopt API · NatLabRockies:REopt_API Wiki_Django Models and Input Validation_0_57

claims.claim_text: In v1/v2, each optimization job consists of four celery tasks (setup_scenario, one optimal and one bau run_jump_model, and process_results). In v3, there is only one celery task per optimization job.
source_chunk_ids: 1. Structure of the REopt API · NatLabRockies:REopt_API Wiki_Django Models and Input Validation_0_57

objective_and_api.summary_abstract: REopt.jl formulates distributed energy resource sizing and dispatch as a mixed-integer linear program minimizing life-cycle cost, with NPV computed as the difference between business-as-usual and optimized LCC. The objective aggregates discounted capital expenditures, operations and maintenance, grid procurement, emissions costs, and expected outage costs—the latter employing a minimax structure that hedges against worst-case outage start times while weighting durations by probability. The Julia API requires specific patterns for scenario ingestion, solver instantiation, result serialization, and parametric sensitivity sweeps.

claims.claim_text: The `to_dict(obj)` utility function recursively converts `JSON.Object` instances returned by `JSON.parsefile` into native Julia `Dict{String,Any}` structures, addressing the documented requirement that REopt internals expect native dictionary types rather than `JSON.Object` wrappers.
source_chunk_ids: 1. Structure of the REopt API · NatLabRockies:REopt_API Wiki_Django Models and Input Validation_0_57

claims.claim_text: The `make_serializable(d::Dict)` function traverses a results dictionary and converts every `JuMP.Containers.DenseAxisArray` to a standard `Array`, enabling downstream `JSON.print` serialization.

The scenario builder must read the baseline `reopt_wwtp_scenario.json` produced by the upstream MATLAB stage (`compute_voll_for_reopt.m`), apply programmatic overrides to selected fields, and validate the resulting dictionary against REopt.jl field expectations.
source_chunk_ids: 1. Structure of the REopt API · NatLabRockies:REopt_API Wiki_Django Models and Input Validation_0_57

claims.claim_text: The override mechanism must support at minimum the following keys: `Financial.value_of_lost_load_per_kwh` (scalar or 8760-element vector), `ElectricUtility.outage_durations`, `ElectricUtility.outage_start_time_steps`, and `ElectricUtility.outage_probabilities`.
VoLL (`Financial.value_of_lost_load_per_kwh`) may be a scalar or an 8760-element vector.

Validation should confirm field types, min/max bounds, and time-series length alignment with `Settings.time_steps_per_hour`, mirroring the `InputValidator.clean_fields()` and `cross_clean()` logic documented in the Django v3 API architecture.
source_chunk_ids: 1. Structure of the REopt API · NatLabRockies:REopt_API Wiki_Django Models and Input Validation_0_57

claims.claim_text: In the Django v3 API, `InputValidator.clean_fields()` validates field types, min/max values, and sets defaults, while `cross_clean()` performs cross-model validation such as ensuring timeseries input lengths align with `Settings.time_steps_per_hour`.
source_chunk_ids: 1. Structure of the REopt API · NatLabRockies:REopt_API Wiki_Django Models and Input Validation_0_57

claims.claim_text: ISO DR revenue is not native to the REopt objective but is computed in post-processing from the solved `ElectricStorage.size_kw` and `Generator.size_kw` values.
DR capacity revenue is computed as R_DR = r_DR × (P_batt + P_gen) × 12, where P_batt is `ElectricStorage.size_kw` and P_gen is `Generator.size_kw` from the results dictionary.
source_chunk_ids: Inputs · REopt.jl Documentation_The replacement strategy cost is:_0_20

claims.claim_text: The adjusted NPV adds DR revenue to the baseline NPV = LCC_BAU - LCC_Optimal.

There is an open decision point on whether the ISO capacity commitment should enforce a floor constraint on `ElectricStorage.size_kw` (e.g., `min_kw >= committed_capacity`) as a JuMP constraint added before calling `run_reopt`.

The primary driver script `run_wwdr.jl` instantiates two `JuMP.Model` instances for the BAU comparison pattern documented in the v3 architecture, where BAU and optimal scenarios run in parallel within the Julia process.
source_chunk_ids: 1. Structure of the REopt API · NatLabRockies:REopt_API Wiki_In v3 of the API we use the REopt Julia package in a similar fashion (see_0_58
1. Structure of the REopt API · NatLabRockies:REopt_API Wiki_Django Models and Input Validation_0_57

claims.claim_text: The driver script invokes the two-model `run_reopt(m1, m2, scenario_dict)` signature.
source_chunk_ids: 1. Structure of the REopt API · NatLabRockies:REopt_API Wiki_Django Models and Input Validation_0_57

claims.claim_text: In v3 of the REopt API, BAU and optimal scenarios are run in parallel by the REopt Julia package, and running the BAU scenario is optional via `Settings.run_bau` input.
source_chunk_ids: 1. Structure of the REopt API · NatLabRockies:REopt_API Wiki_In v3 of the API we use the REopt Julia package in a similar fashion (see_0_58

claims.claim_text: The solver models should be configured with `set_attribute(m, "time_limit", 600.0)`.
The REopt.jl package can be used with just a few lines of Julia code without accessing the REopt API, using `run_reopt(m, "path/to/scenario.json")`.
source_chunk_ids: 1. Structure of the REopt API · NatLabRockies:REopt_API Wiki_Django Models and Input Validation_0_57

claims.claim_text: The VoLL sensitivity sweep executes a parametric sweep over VoLL scalar multipliers ranging from 0.1× to 10× the marginal VoLL baseline, following a deepcopy-override-solve-collect pattern.
VoLL may be a scalar or an 8760-element vector; the multiplier scales whichever form is present.
The `value_of_lost_load_per_kwh` field in the Financial input can be either a scalar Real or an Array of Real values.

The outage duration and probability sweep varies the duration vector (e.g., [4, 8, 16, 24, 48, 72, 168] hours) and the associated probability weights, using the stochastic outage formulation that evaluates resilience across a combinatorial grid of start times and durations.
The outage sweep outputs include `expected_outage_cost`, `resilience_hours_min`, `resilience_hours_avg`, and `probs_of_surviving`.
REopt's `outage_probabilities` input accepts a vector aligned with `outage_durations`.
The `simulate_outages` function returns `resilience_hours_min`, `resilience_hours_max`, `resilience_hours_avg`, `probs_of_surviving`, and `probs_of_surviving_by_month`.

The NPV decomposition must include component terms: capital expenditure (net of ITC and MACRS), O&M lifecycle costs, grid energy and demand charges, fuel costs, export revenues, production incentives, battery replacement or augmentation costs, battery residual value, expected outage costs, and DR capacity revenue.
source_chunk_ids: Inputs · REopt.jl Documentation_The replacement strategy cost is:_0_20

claims.claim_text: The NPV decomposition must satisfy the identity: NPV_adjusted = LCC_BAU - LCC_Optimal + R_DR,lifecycle.
The DER sizing summary table should include `PV.size_kw`, `ElectricStorage.size_kw`, `ElectricStorage.size_kwh`, `Generator.size_kw`, and any CHP or thermal storage capacities.
source_chunk_ids: Inputs · REopt.jl Documentation_The replacement strategy cost is:_0_20

claims.claim_text: The `Outages` results section is only added to results when outages are modeled via the `ElectricUtility.outage_start_time_steps` and `ElectricUtility.outage_durations` inputs.
The `expected_outage_cost` is a key output in the REopt Outages results.
In v3 of the REopt API, there is only one celery task per optimization job, accomplished by running the BAU and optimal scenarios in parallel in the Julia code.
source_chunk_ids: 1. Structure of the REopt API · NatLabRockies:REopt_API Wiki_Django Models and Input Validation_0_57

claims.claim_text: Battery residual value captures the dollar value of remaining battery life at end of analysis period and is added to the objective function.
source_chunk_ids: Inputs · REopt.jl Documentation_The replacement strategy cost is:_0_20

claims.claim_text: The battery replacement strategy cost is added to the objective function to be minimized with all other costs.
source_chunk_ids: Inputs · REopt.jl Documentation_The replacement strategy cost is:_0_20

claims.claim_text: The NPV for financial analysis is calculated as the difference between the Business-As-Usual Case life cycle energy cost and the optimal case life cycle energy cost.
The `utils_to_dict.jl` file is consumed by all other files via `include("utils_to_dict.jl")`.
source_chunk_ids: 1. Structure of the REopt API · NatLabRockies:REopt_API Wiki_Django Models and Input Validation_0_57

claims.claim_text: The run_wwdr.jl driver script requires packages `REopt`, `JuMP`, `HiGHS`, and `JSON`.
source_chunk_ids: 1. Structure of the REopt API · NatLabRockies:REopt_API Wiki_Django Models and Input Validation_0_57

claims.claim_text: The `simulate_outages` function performs time series simulation of outages starting at every time step of the year, used to calculate how many time steps the critical load can be met in every outage, which determines probabilities of meeting the critical load.
Outage timeseries results are 3-dimensional arrays with dimensions corresponding to the outage durations, the outage start time, and the time step in the outage.

The inter-file dependency graph shows `utils_to_dict.jl` as the root dependency consumed by `scenario_builder.jl` and the driver scripts, with `postprocess.jl` as the terminal node with no downstream consumers.
source_chunk_ids: 1. Structure of the REopt API · NatLabRockies:REopt_API Wiki_Migration of Python code to Julia_0_59

claims.claim_text: The hourly VoLL profile's time-varying magnitude creates an asymmetry with the coarser `outage_durations` scenario granularity, and when a VoLL vector is scaled by a uniform multiplier, the interaction with discrete outage duration bins may produce non-monotonic expected-outage-cost responses.

In v1/v2 of the REopt API, each optimization job consists of four celery tasks (`setup_scenario`, one optimal and one bau `run_jump_model`, and `process_results`), while in v3 there is only one celery task per optimization job.
source_chunk_ids: 1. Structure of the REopt API · NatLabRockies:REopt_API Wiki_Django Models and Input Validation_0_57

claims.claim_text: The `build_scenario` function signature is `function build_scenario(json_path::String; overrides::Dict=Dict()) -> Dict{String,Any}`.
source_chunk_ids: 1. Structure of the REopt API · NatLabRockies:REopt_API Wiki_Django Models and Input Validation_0_57

claims.claim_text: The VoLL sweep grid consists of multipliers [0.1, 0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 1.0, 1.5, 2.0, 3.0, 5.0, 7.5, 10.0].

Each VoLL sweep iteration follows the deepcopy-override-solve-collect pattern: deep-copy the base scenario, override `Financial.value_of_lost_load_per_kwh`, instantiate a fresh solver model per iteration, execute using the two-model signature, and serialize results.
source_chunk_ids: 1. Structure of the REopt API · NatLabRockies:REopt_API Wiki_Django Models and Input Validation_0_57

claims.claim_text: Three open decision points are identified: (1) whether ISO capacity commitment should enforce a `min_kw` floor on `ElectricStorage.size_kw` as a JuMP constraint, (2) how to reconcile hourly VoLL vector granularity with coarser `outage_durations` bins, and (3) whether seasonal outage probability weighting should replace uniform weighting.

Much of the inputs setup code and post-processing has shifted from the API to the Julia package in v3, meaning the JuMP model and all supporting functions are in a separate repository and can be used with just a few lines of Julia code.
source_chunk_ids: 1. Structure of the REopt API · NatLabRockies:REopt_API Wiki_Migration of Python code to Julia_0_59
1. Structure of the REopt API · NatLabRockies:REopt_API Wiki_Django Models and Input Validation_0_57

implementation_roadmap.summary_abstract: This section presents a detailed file-by-file implementation roadmap for a Julia-based wastewater treatment plant demand response analysis using the REopt.jl framework, specifying seven interconnected modules: recursive type conversion utilities, scenario ingestion and validation, ISO demand response revenue post-processing, a primary driver script, parametric sweeps over value of lost load and outage duration/probability, and a post-processing module for NPV decomposition and resilience metrics reporting. The roadmap delineates each file's responsibilities, function signatures, inputs, outputs, and inter-file dependencies, while identifying three unresolved design decisions regarding capacity commitment constraint injection, hourly VoLL–outage duration reconciliation, and seasonal outage probability weighting.
